title: July 28th, 2022
body:
https://open.spotify.com/

agenda

no events scheduled for today!

possibilities

scratch paper

queries/todos

queries/tweets in progress

todone

game design

less wizards, more magic

no wizards, just magic

the main prerogative is creating a healthy magic ecosystem minimally dependent on wizards commercial pipeline

donated cards provided to and collected from players at the event

enforced by tracking pairings of players, boxes, and tables

only publicly donated prizes? no prizes?

what do prizes do in terms of anchoring community to or priming it for the wizards ecosystem? we want to inoculate potential gamers by showing them an alternative to toxic practices

can we leverage public donors to build a coalition?

is this primarily an offramp from wizards' magic?

is it ok if it ends up as an onramp that seeds certain ideas into noobs that pass through?

can it function as a bulwark for people curious about card games and prone to end up predated

to anyone who wonders if this format isn't a parasite on wizards that can't survive without it, point to chess and poker as surviving past their creator's names even

we are quite happy to contribute to the failure of wizards of the coast as a business enterprise

players sign up for a color trio

each eight-person table gets a sleeve of donated cards of the chosen shard or wedge to build eight decks with

time-blocked event

registration and mingle

build

battle

play as much as you can

playing more does not benefit heavily over a player with a similar winning percentage in fewer games. but it does make some difference

for example, if a win = 200 and a loss = -190 then a player who went 10-0 would have as many points as a player who went 20-20, 15-10

end of regulation ceremony

all the collected celebrations

final face-off

untimed-ish

there's no room for extras

table-mates buddy up into pairs

pairs can trade cards between matches

i like the incentive to keep track of at least one of your table mates

is there any opportunity to trade cards in match or even in game

what individual and collective accomplishments can be tracked and celebrated for the event

total number of games played

cheeky reference to total number of wins, losses, and draws

by player, pair, table, and color set

most wins

most points

wins = 100

losses = -90

most matches

least matches

color set only

top two tables at end of regulation/overtime pick two pairs each to play a four-on-four knockout

lose your match, you're out. last team with a survivor wins

are slow decks strategically enticing in that you can guarantee surviving more clock time and seeing what happens from there?

battle against players from other tables in an all day free for all

lyrics

i love working downhill
but first i gotta get up
it's ill
cuz both could kill
but only one test your will
it's silly i still worry what could kill me
but really i'm dying on the tongue
lying in the comfort that i ain't the one

identeractivity

i came i saw i conquered

mutativity

what dreams may 1st, 2022 come

matereality

it is what it is

daily instagram post



caption

painted this pair of 16x20 canvases in [@coolioboolio](https://www.instagram.com/coolioboolio/) and ryan's basement while visiting my dad last year. his health was failing and i'm so grateful that in the middle of all that, i had a place i could relax enough to make art and keep my shit moving

the original paintings are hanging up in our house now. for sale: $640 for the pair, $400 each

if you'd rather order a one-off print, dm me here. custom edits, iterations, and remixes are available on request*

morning tweet

https://twitter.com/a_k_a_billy/status/1552731065026023427

i read the forgotten realms avatar series while it was releasing, back during my fantasy-crushing teens. seriously, i read so much someone could dig up this tweet in a time of apocalyptic need and mistake me for a giant killer. turned out i was a tailor the whole time



and i admire how way this series wove together world-building material from a deep line-up of authors by intimately focusing on a group of special friends who's small insanities changed everything


building on fantasy and comic book tropes ambitiously, this was a heftier investment for readers, writers, and world/brand. the consequences felt heavy and meaningfully non-reversible. and boy did things change



gods, having been cast out of the heavenly realms but a disappointed universe, kill, die, and otherwise adjust to life in the material world. two members of the original adventuring party ascend, the first through fate and duty, the second through murderous ambition
